strengths: Hands-on AI-agent infrastructure experience at re:cinq, building MCP code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows — directly relevant to the agentic-AI mandate

Deep distributed-systems and low-latency backend expertise: microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O, caching, and real-time metadata at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, plus an event-driven geo-data platform (GISCollective) he founded and operates on Kubernetes

Fintech and pricing-domain exposure: reshaped investment products at RIDE Capital with disciplined engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based), and optimized multi-country pricing strategies at Optilyz
concerns: No formal executive or CTO-level role; leadership evidence is technical (staff/senior IC, founder, workshops) rather than organizational
No demonstrated track record scaling an engineering org from 10 to 30+ people or developing a senior leadership layer
Production multi-agent AI experience is recent and brief (~6 months at re:cinq), short of the JD's bar for operating multi-agent systems in high-stakes environments

key_factors.factor: Agentic-AI tooling and MCP infrastructure experience

response.justification: Bogdan brings nearly two decades of engineering experience spanning exactly the technical pillars this role rests on: recent, hands-on agentic-AI infrastructure work (MCP generators, agent frameworks), proven low-latency event-driven systems at extreme scale, and fintech product delivery in Germany's regulated investment space. As a founder he has independently architected, operated, and sustained a production event-driven platform for a decade, demonstrating ownership and systems judgment beyond his IC titles. His craftsmanship orientation — TDD, CI/CD, observability, teaching and mentoring through courses and dojos — maps well to the JD's emphasis on evaluation rigor, production reliability, and developing technical leaders, making him a credible technical foundation for an agentic-fintech platform even as his executive scope would need to grow into the role.
